blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: I was about to call you.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: The IP. Charlotte. One person in that group is from Charlotte.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Don't say it. Not yet. Not on a call.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: You already had a direction before this.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I've had a direction for two days. This is the first data point that isn't behavioral.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: But you still won't put a name on it.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: A name without a case just gives the wrong person time to prepare. I need the tox. I need whatever your divers find at the jetty. And I need Wren.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: She's going to come to you today. She's been carrying something since Saturday night and she's almost ready to say it. Don't push her toward a name — let her get there on her own. If she names someone herself it's far stronger than if we lead her there.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: And in the meantime nothing changes in how we handle the house.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Nothing. This person has been patient for a very long time. If they sense the focus shifting they'll have thought through that too.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: How do you know Wren spoke to someone last night.
